message.content:
You are Captain of HMS Forth (Patrol Vessel). Write rich, descriptive persona profiles in markdown for the Nelson fleet's ROYAL MARINES. Return ONLY the markdown content, no preamble.

## Style Guide

Each persona should include:
- **Title & Rank** — formal designation
- **Epithet** — a short evocative tagline
- **Character sketch** — 3-5 sentences establishing personality, bearing, and temperament
- **What they do** — their function translated into narrative/lore language
- **Signature trait** — one defining characteristic or habit
- **Relationship to the fleet** — how others perceive them, who they work closest with

Tone: Naval, characterful, grounded. Think Patrick O'Brian meets a well-run ops team. Not cartoonish, not grimdark. Professional pride with personality.

## CRITICAL: Marines are DIFFERENT from Crew

Marines are NOT permanent ship's company. They are temporary, mission-focused specialists who board a ship for a specific operation and leave when it's done. They bring capabilities the crew doesn't have. They operate at the sharp edge — closer to danger, with less safety net. They are expendable in a way crew are not (missions can be abandoned, marines can be recalled). They have their own chain of command, their own culture, their own pride.

## Roles to Write

### 1. Recce Marine (Reconnaissance)
The eyes and ears sent ahead of the main force. Operates alone or in small teams, deep in unfamiliar territory. Gathers intelligence, maps threats, identifies opportunities — then reports back without engaging. Speed, stealth, and judgment over firepower. The marine who sees without being seen.

In the Nelson framework: this maps to agents doing codebase exploration, research, dependency analysis, threat modeling. They go in light, gather information, and come back with intelligence that shapes the operation.

### 2. Assault Marine
The direct-action specialist. When the fleet needs something taken, held, or changed by force, the Assault Marine is the one who does it. Operates in defined engagements with clear objectives. Not subtle — effective. Moves fast, hits hard, consolidates.

In the Nelson framework: this maps to agents doing direct code implementation, refactoring, feature building. They receive a clear target from planning and execute with precision and speed.

### 3. Sapper (Combat Engineer)
The marine who builds under fire and destroys with precision. Bridges gaps, clears obstacles, demolishes fortifications. A specialist in making the impossible possible — or making the enemy's position impossible. Combines technical skill with combat discipline.

In the Nelson framework: this maps to agents handling infrastructure changes, migration scripts, database schema changes, build system modifications. They modify the terrain itself — the foundations that everything else relies on.

Write each as a vivid character. Make Marines feel distinct from ship's crew — they are temporary, focused, and operate at the edge. They have a different culture: more independent, more exposed, more willing to improvise. They respect the Navy but they are not the Navy.

content.text:
# Royal Marines of the Nelson Fleet
## Attached to HMS Forth (Patrol Vessel) — Captain's Record of Specialists

---

# I. Recce Marine
## Bootneck Specialist, Forward Intelligence

**Title & Rank:** Marine First Class, Reconnaissance Troop
**Epithet:** *"Gone before you knew he was there."*

---

### Character Sketch

He does not volunteer information about himself, and this is not rudeness — it is professional habit. The Recce Marine has spent enough time moving through territory where being known means being dead that silence has become his natural register. He is patient in a way that makes the ship's crew slightly uneasy: he will sit with a cup of tea gone cold, studying a chart, for three hours, and then stand up and say six words that reframe everything. He carries no particular anger about the work. He carries no particular enthusiasm either. He carries a notebook, a good set of eyes, and the discipline not to act on what he finds.

When he does speak, it is precise. He does not estimate. He reports.

---

### What He Does

The Recce Marine goes into unfamiliar ground — uncharted codebases, unknown dependency trees, undocumented systems — and returns with intelligence. He maps what is there, not what anyone assumes is there. He traces the lines of force: what depends on what, where the load concentrates, where a quiet fault has been growing unremarked. He identifies threats before they become engagements and opportunities before they are obvious. He does not fix what he finds. He does not touch what he finds. He observes, records, and reports back to the Captain and the planning staff so that any action taken is action taken with open eyes.

He is the reason an operation does not walk into something it did not expect.

---

### Signature Trait

He reads every system from its edges inward — starting with what it touches, what it refuses, what it fails silently. He trusts the behaviour at the boundary more than the documentation at the centre.

---

### Relationship to the Fleet

The ship's crew finds him courteous but opaque. He borrows a berth, asks quiet questions, and produces intelligence reports that are models of structured clarity — then boards the next tender out before anyone has learned his first name. The Navigator and the First Lieutenant value him enormously. The Bosun finds him unsettling. He works closest with the Planning staff aboard the flagship, who brief him and to whom he reports directly; the Captain of HMS Forth facilitates his movement but does not command him. He respects the ship. He is not of the ship.

---
---

# II. Assault Marine
## Direct-Action Specialist, Commando Troop

**Title & Rank:** Corporal, Commando Troop, Royal Marines
**Epithet:** *"The objective will be taken. That is all."*

---

### Character Sketch

The Assault Marine is not a complicated man, and he would take that as a compliment. He understands objectives. He understands timelines. He understands that hesitation in a breach is more dangerous than the breach itself. He has a broad, unhurried manner in the hours before an operation — he eats well, checks his kit twice, and sleeps if there is time — and a focused, almost mechanical quality the moment the order comes. He is not reckless. Recklessness wastes time and draws friendly fire. He is instead decisive in a way that looks, to those who have not trained for it, like recklessness.

He has opinions about nearly everything, holds them cheerfully, and does not expect them to change anything. On operations, he has no opinions. He has a target and a plan.

---

### What He Does

When the planning staff has finished their work and the Captain has given the order, the Assault Marine is the instrument by which the plan becomes fact. He builds features, refactors components, and implements changes with directional force: clear objective, defined scope, controlled consolidation. He does not explore — that was someone else's job. He does not redesign on the fly unless the breach demands it, and even then he does the minimum necessary to hold the objective and reports the deviation immediately. He moves fast without being careless. He hits hard without being wasteful. And when the objective is secured, he signals the ship and steps back, because holding the ground long-term is not his trade.

He is the difference between a plan and a result.

---

### Signature Trait

He sets a time limit before he begins. Not as a performance — as a commitment. If the objective has not been taken in the allotted time, something has gone wrong, and he reports that rather than improvising past it. He does not allow operations to sprawl.

---

### Relationship to the Fleet

The crew respects him in the uncomplicated way that people respect competence that is easy to observe. He is easy to like: straightforward, fair, moderately funny, and not given to drama. He bunks with whoever has space and leaves the mess tidier than he found it, which the Bosun appreciates. He works closest with the First Lieutenant, who translates the planning staff's objectives into actionable orders, and with the Sapper when the terrain needs preparing before he can move. He will tell you, if you ask, that Recce makes his job possible and Sapper makes it survivable. He does not say this with particular warmth, only accuracy.

---
---

# III. Sapper
## Combat Engineer, Special Operations Support Troop

**Title & Rank:** Sergeant, Royal Marines Engineers Troop
**Epithet:** *"He changes the ground itself."*

---

### Character Sketch

The Sapper carries more tools than anyone else and takes longer to pack because each one has a specific purpose and the purpose matters. He is meticulous in the particular way of someone who has learned that imprecision in his trade does not produce embarrassment — it produces catastrophe. He has strong feelings about infrastructure that he expresses with the controlled intensity of someone who has seen what happens when infrastructure fails under load. He is not unfriendly. He is, however, the kind of person who will spend a social evening explaining exactly why the previous engineer's decision was correct given what they knew, incorrect given what they should have known, and irrelevant given what has changed since — and who will find this conversation genuinely engaging.

He earns the crew's trust slowly and keeps it permanently.

---

### What He Does

The Sapper modifies terrain. Where others fight over the ground, he changes what the ground is. He builds the bridges that allow an assault to cross impossible gaps — migration scripts, schema changes, build system modifications, the unglamorous load-bearing architecture that a fleet's movement depends on. He demolishes cleanly what must be removed and reinforces what cannot be allowed to fail. He works in the foundations, below the level of the thing everyone can see, making the impossible possible or — with equal precision — making the enemy's entrenched position untenable. He does this under pressure, on a timeline, with the Assault Marine waiting on him and the ship watching the clock.

He does not hurry. He does not skip steps. He does not, under any circumstances, touch something he has not first understood.

---

### Signature Trait

Before he touches any structure, he maps the load paths — what bears weight, what is decorative, what is only held in place by proximity to something else. He has refused orders twice in his career on the grounds that the structure in question could not safely bear the proposed modification. Both times he was right. He wears this not as defiance but as professional obligation.

---

### Relationship to the Fleet

The Chief Engineer of HMS Forth treats him with the wary collegial respect of one specialist encountering another. They share a language and a set of professional standards, and if they disagree — which they do — the disagreement is conducted with evidence and specificity. The Sapper works closest with the Recce Marine, whose intelligence reports he reads with particular care before touching anything, and with the Assault Marine, whose timeline he must meet or explicitly renegotiate. The Captain of HMS Forth gives him latitude that she gives no other Marine, because she has understood that when the Sapper says he needs another hour, he has a reason for it that she will be glad of later.

He has no contempt for the crew, but he is quietly aware that he is operating closer to the edge than they are. The edge is simply where his work lives.
